com.newland.nsdk.core.api.internal.pinentry

Enum PINPadButton

  • java.lang.Object
    • java.lang.Enum<PINPadButton>
      • com.newland.nsdk.core.api.internal.pinentry.PINPadButton
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.io.Serializable, java.lang.Comparable<PINPadButton>

    public enum PINPadButton
extends java.lang.Enum<PINPadButton>
    PIN pad buttons.

    • Enum Constant Summary

      Enum Constants 
      Enum Constant and Description
      BACKSPACE
      Indicates the BACKSPACE button.
      BLANK1
      This button is used for filling the key layout.
      BLANK2
      This button is used for filling the key layout.
      CANCEL
      Indicates the CANCEL button.
      CLEAR
      This button will only clear the entered password.
      ENTER
      Indicates the ENTER button.
      NUMBER_0
      Indicates the button to display the first number of number buffer returned by PINEntry.initKeyLayout(Map, boolean)
      NUMBER_1
      Indicates the button to display the second number of number buffer returned by PINEntry.initKeyLayout(Map, boolean)
      NUMBER_2
      Indicates the button to display the 3th number of number buffer returned by PINEntry.initKeyLayout(Map, boolean)
      NUMBER_3
      Indicates the button to display the 4th number of number buffer returned by PINEntry.initKeyLayout(Map, boolean)
      NUMBER_4
      Indicates the button to display the 5th number of number buffer returned by PINEntry.initKeyLayout(Map, boolean)
      NUMBER_5
      Indicates the button to display the 6th number of number buffer returned by PINEntry.initKeyLayout(Map, boolean)
      NUMBER_6
      Indicates the button to display the 7th number of number buffer returned by PINEntry.initKeyLayout(Map, boolean)
      NUMBER_7
      Indicates the button to display the 8th number of number buffer returned by PINEntry.initKeyLayout(Map, boolean)
      NUMBER_8
      Indicates the button to display the 9th number of number buffer returned by PINEntry.initKeyLayout(Map, boolean)
      NUMBER_9
      Indicates the button to display the 10th number of number buffer returned by PINEntry.initKeyLayout(Map, boolean)
      QUIT
      This button will cancel PIN input directly without clearing the entered password.

      Returns the enum constant of this type with the specified name.

      Returns an array containing the constants of this enum type, in the order they are declared.

    • Enum Constant Detail

      • NUMBER_0

        public static final PINPadButton NUMBER_0
        Indicates the button to display the first number of number buffer returned by PINEntry.initKeyLayout(Map, boolean)

      • NUMBER_1

        public static final PINPadButton NUMBER_1
        Indicates the button to display the second number of number buffer returned by PINEntry.initKeyLayout(Map, boolean)

      • NUMBER_2

        public static final PINPadButton NUMBER_2
        Indicates the button to display the 3th number of number buffer returned by PINEntry.initKeyLayout(Map, boolean)

      • NUMBER_3

        public static final PINPadButton NUMBER_3
        Indicates the button to display the 4th number of number buffer returned by PINEntry.initKeyLayout(Map, boolean)

      • NUMBER_4

        public static final PINPadButton NUMBER_4
        Indicates the button to display the 5th number of number buffer returned by PINEntry.initKeyLayout(Map, boolean)

      • NUMBER_5

        public static final PINPadButton NUMBER_5
        Indicates the button to display the 6th number of number buffer returned by PINEntry.initKeyLayout(Map, boolean)

      • NUMBER_6

        public static final PINPadButton NUMBER_6
        Indicates the button to display the 7th number of number buffer returned by PINEntry.initKeyLayout(Map, boolean)

      • NUMBER_7

        public static final PINPadButton NUMBER_7
        Indicates the button to display the 8th number of number buffer returned by PINEntry.initKeyLayout(Map, boolean)

      • NUMBER_8

        public static final PINPadButton NUMBER_8
        Indicates the button to display the 9th number of number buffer returned by PINEntry.initKeyLayout(Map, boolean)

      • NUMBER_9

        public static final PINPadButton NUMBER_9
        Indicates the button to display the 10th number of number buffer returned by PINEntry.initKeyLayout(Map, boolean)

      • ENTER

        public static final PINPadButton ENTER
        Indicates the ENTER button.

      • BACKSPACE

        public static final PINPadButton BACKSPACE
        Indicates the BACKSPACE button.

      • CANCEL

        public static final PINPadButton CANCEL
        Indicates the CANCEL button.
        • If user has entered any password, it will clear the entered password on first click, exit PIN input on second click.
        • If there is no password entered, it will cancel PIN input on first click.

      • CLEAR

        public static final PINPadButton CLEAR
        This button will only clear the entered password.

      • QUIT

        public static final PINPadButton QUIT
        This button will cancel PIN input directly without clearing the entered password.

      • BLANK1

        public static final PINPadButton BLANK1
        This button is used for filling the key layout.

      • BLANK2

        public static final PINPadButton BLANK2
        This button is used for filling the key layout.

    • Method Detail

      • values

        public static PINPadButton[] values()
        Returns an array containing the constants of this enum type, in the order they are declared. This method may be used to iterate over the constants as follows: 
        for (PINPadButton c : PINPadButton.values())
    System.out.println(c);
        Returns:
        an array containing the constants of this enum type, in the order they are declared

      • valueOf

        public static PINPadButton valueOf(java.lang.String name)
        Returns the enum constant of this type with the specified name. The string must match exactly an identifier used to declare an enum constant in this type. (Extraneous whitespace characters are not permitted.)
        Parameters:
        name - the name of the enum constant to be returned.
        Returns:
        the enum constant with the specified name
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if this enum type has no constant with the specified name
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if the argument is null
